summary: DHH notes the irony that despite creating a famous demo showing Rails can build a blog in 10 minutes, his own blog still runs on an aging Movable Type installation. He highlights other Rails developers who have already built and deployed their own Rails-powered blogs, praising their designs and features. He expresses hope that one of them will open source their blogging system so he can use it instead of building his own.
key_points: DHH's own blog still runs on outdated Movable Type despite Rails being capable of replacing it in minutes
Early Rails community members were already building and shipping real applications with the framework
He hopes for an open source Rails blogging system to emerge from the community rather than building one himself
The post captures the early days of Rails adoption and community formation
money_quotes: Loud Thinking is still chugging along on a Moveable Type installation that is years old.
It's one of those always-on-the-todo-list kind of things.
Thankfully, not all Rails developers are as slow as me to get their weblog running on Rails.
Hopefully Sarah (or Marten) will get around to open sourcing of her system, so there's a base to offset my eternal procrastination in getting my blogging on Rails going.
key_insight: Even the creator of Rails procrastinated on migrating his own blog, illustrating that the cobbler's children go barefoot — but also that a healthy community was already forming around the framework.
